name: UniversalDataReader
shortDescription: Reads data from flat files (CSV, fixed-length).
description: Reads data from delimited or fixed length files and sends data records to first connected output port. If any error occurs, the report is sent to the second port if "Data policy" is set to "Controlled". This component is alias for FlatFileReader.

properties.name: fileURL
properties.label: File URL
properties.description: Specifies the path or URL of the input file to read data from. This is the primary attribute that determines which file will be processed by the component.

properties.name: charset
properties.label: Charset
properties.description: Defines the character encoding used to decode the input file content. If not specified, the system will attempt to detect the encoding automatically.

properties.name: dataPolicy
properties.label: Data policy
properties.description: Controls how the component handles data parsing errors and malformed records during processing.

properties.name: skipFirstLine
properties.label: Skip first line
properties.description: When enabled, skips the first line of the input file, commonly used to ignore header rows in CSV files.

properties.name: trim
properties.label: Trim strings
properties.description: When enabled, removes leading and trailing whitespace from string field values during parsing.

properties.name: quotedStrings
properties.label: Quoted strings
properties.description: Enables parsing of quoted string values in delimited files, allowing strings to contain delimiter characters.

properties.name: quoteCharacter
properties.label: Quote character
properties.description: Specifies the character used to quote string values in delimited files when quoted strings are enabled.

properties.name: skipLeadingBlanks
properties.label: Skip leading blanks
properties.description: When enabled, ignores leading whitespace characters before field values during parsing.

properties.name: skipTrailingBlanks
properties.label: Skip trailing blanks
properties.description: When enabled, ignores trailing whitespace characters after field values during parsing.

properties.name: skipRows
properties.label: Number of skipped records
properties.description: Specifies the number of records to skip from the beginning of the file before starting to process data.

properties.name: numRecords
properties.label: Max number of records
properties.description: Limits the maximum number of records to read from the input file. If not specified, all records are processed.

properties.name: skipSourceRows
properties.label: Number of skipped records per source
properties.description: When processing multiple input sources, specifies the number of records to skip at the beginning of each source file.

properties.name: numSourceRecords
properties.label: Max number of records per source
properties.description: When processing multiple input sources, limits the maximum number of records to read from each source file.

properties.name: missingColumnHandling
properties.label: Missing column handling
properties.description: Defines how the component handles missing columns in the input data compared to the expected metadata structure.

properties.name: maxErrorCount
properties.label: Max error count
properties.description: Sets the maximum number of parsing errors allowed before the component stops processing. Unlimited if not specified.

properties.name: treatMultipleDelimitersAsOne
properties.label: Treat multiple delimiters as one
properties.description: When enabled, treats consecutive delimiter characters as a single delimiter, useful for handling inconsistent spacing.

properties.name: incrementalFile
properties.label: Incremental file
properties.description: Specifies a file used to store incremental processing state, enabling the component to resume from where it left off.

properties.name: incrementalKey
properties.label: Incremental key
properties.description: Defines the key field used for incremental processing to track the last processed record position.

properties.name: verbose
properties.label: Verbose
properties.description: When enabled, provides detailed logging information about the data reading process for debugging purposes.

properties.name: parser
properties.label: Parser
properties.description: Specifies the parser implementation to use for reading the input data, with automatic selection as the default.

name: SimpleCopy
description: Receives data through connected input port and copies data records to all connected output ports.
usage:
When to use: Broadcast one input stream to multiple output ports unchanged — every record goes to ALL connected outputs simultaneously. Use when the same data needs to feed multiple downstream branches in parallel (e.g. write to DB and to a CSV file at the same time, or feed two different transformation chains with the same source records).
Constraints: All connected output ports receive every record — there is no content-based routing. Use PARTITION if different records should go to different ports. Use REFORMAT if the copies need to be transformed differently per output.

name: Concatenate
description: Receives data through all connected input ports in turn. Gets all records from each input port, sends them to output port and continues with the next input port. Skips ports without incoming data. Terminates when all incoming records are received and sent to connected output port.
usage:
When to use: Merge N streams where output must preserve port order — all records from port 0 first, then all from port 1, etc. Order within each stream is preserved.
Constraints: Slower than SIMPLE_GATHER — waits for each port to finish before moving to the next. Input does NOT need to be sorted.

name: SimpleGather
description: Receives data incoming through all connected input ports in the order so that all records are gathered and send out through output port as soon as possible.
usage:
When to use: Merge N streams where output order doesn't matter. Fastest option — emits records from whichever port has data ready.
Constraints: Output order is non-deterministic. Do NOT use if downstream requires sorted or port-ordered output.

name: Map
shortDescription: Maps (transforms) data from one input to one or multiple outputs. Replaces Reformat component.
description: Receives data through connected input port, transforms them in a user specified way, and sends such new outgoing data records to all connected output ports. Implements org.jetel.component.RecordTransform interface.
usage:
When to use: 1->1 transformation where each input record produces one (modified, enriched, or restructured) output record. Primary use is to transform data structure or content between input and output metadata. Optionally, can produce records on different output ports to support routing or different output structures; return a port number (or ALL) to control routing.
Constraints: Not intended for data-driven record expansion or aggregation across multiple input records. Use NORMALIZER when multiple output records per input are required. Use DENORMALIZER when a single output record should be created from a group of multiple input records.

name: DatabaseWriter
shortDescription: Or DBWriter, replaces DBOutputTable. Writes data into database using JDBC driver. Supported databases are Amazon Redshift, Microsoft Access, Microsoft SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Sybase, Vertica, DB2 and any other database with JDBC compliant driver.
description: Receives data through connected input port, connects to database using JDBC driver, performs database operation and loads data records into specified DB table. Correspondence between input metadata and DB table columns must be defined.
usage:
When to use: Write records from a data stream into a database table via JDBC. Three configuration modes — choose based on how closely field names match column names and how much SQL control is needed:
- dbTable (auto-mapping): Field names in metadata exactly match DB column names. CloverDX auto-generates the INSERT. Simplest option — no SQL required. Use when schema alignment is clean.
- sqlQuery (manual SQL): Full control over the INSERT statement. Field-to-column mapping syntax: $cloverField:=dbColumn. Use when field names differ from column names, or when custom SQL behaviour is needed (ON CONFLICT, MERGE, stored procedure call, etc.).
- url: External .sql file containing the query. Use when the SQL is long or shared across multiple graphs.

Performance: Enable batchMode=true with appropriate batchSize (default 25) for significantly faster bulk loads. Tune commit interval (default 100) to balance transaction overhead vs memory.
Constraints: Requires a defined dbConnection. Port 0 (rejected) receives rows that failed DB insertion when maxErrors > 0. Port 1 (autogenerated) receives DB-generated key values (e.g. auto-increment IDs) when the DB returns them.

name: Dedup
description: Receives sorted data records through connected input port and removes records that are in duplicate with a view to the specified key values. Keeps defined number of records from either the start (First) or the end (Last) of each group with the same key value. If desired, only unique records are kept. Dedup key is name or combination of names of field(s) of incoming records. Rejected records are sent to the optional second output port if connected.
usage:
When to use: Remove duplicate records from a stream based on key field values. Keeps the first, last, or a defined count of records per duplicate group. Optionally routes duplicates to a second output port for inspection or logging rather than silently discarding them. If no dedupKey is defined, the component operates on the entire input as a single group, effectively acting as a global head/tail (first/last N records).

The groups are defined by previous sorting of the records by the group key, with each group consisting of records considered equal when sorting by the group key. Sends sorted data records to all connected output ports. Sort key (as well as group key) is name or combination of names of field(s) of incoming data records. name: Aggregate
description: Receives data through connected input port, aggregates information about groups of adjacent records with the same aggregate key value, creates outgoing records from this information along with other input fields according to output metadata and sends these resulting records to all connected output ports. Can by used similarly as GROUP BY in SQL.
usage:
When to use: Standard GROUP BY — sum, avg, count, min, max, first, last on groups. No custom logic needed. Fastest option for standard aggregations.
Constraints: Only built-in aggregation functions. Input sorted=true (default) requires pre-sorted input; sorted=false handles unsorted but uses more memory.
Prefer over ROLLUP when built-in functions are sufficient.

properties.name: aggregateKey
properties.label: Aggregate key
properties.description: Defines the key fields used for aggregate key.

properties.name: mapping
properties.label: Aggregation mapping
properties.description: Configures aggregation mapping. Example: $unit_price:=min($unit_price);$unit_amount:=avg($unit_amount,\"$unit_price > 10\");$total:=sum($total);$item_location:=$item_location;. Keys can be mapped directly. Aggregation functions include count, sum, min, max, avg, first, last, countnonnull, countunique, firstnonnull, lastnonnull, median, modus, stdev. Count* functions do not take field as parameter (put space). For all functions, an optional CTL condition can be provided in quotes to filter records. Constants can be mapped using $CONST:=value, where value can be string (\"ABC\"), integer (123), decimal (123.45), boolean (true/false), date (2012-01-01), or datetime (2020-01-01 12:20:20). Multiple mappings are separated by semicolon.

name: ApproximativeJoin
description: Receives data through two input ports, for each matching key value contained in driver data records (port 0) searches corresponding slave data records (port 1) with the same key value. For every pair of driver and slave records conformity based on join key is computed as Levenstein distance. Pairs with conformity higher than specified value are joined to form outgoing data flow and these resulting records are sent to the first output port. Pairs with conformity less are joined to form outgoing data flow and these resulting records sent to the second output port. Driver records without slave are sent to the third output port if connected. Slave records without driver are sent to the fourth output port if connected.

name: DBJoin
shortDescription: Joins incoming data flow with records from database table.
description: Receives data through connected input port (drivers), connects to database, for each join key value contained in driver data records searches corresponding data records with the same key value in database table (slaves). Every pair of driver and slave records is sent to transformation class that joins them to form outgoing data flow. Resulting data records are sent to the first output port. Driver records without slave are sent to the second output port if connected and if inner join (default) is performed.
usage:
When to use: Join driver stream against a database table via SQL query per record. Slave data lives in DB, not in memory. Use when slave is in a DB and too large for a lookup table, or when slave data must be current (real-time DB lookup per record).
Constraints: One DB query per driver record (or per cache miss) — slow for large driver streams. maxCached (default 100) caches recent results. For bulk joins, prefer loading DB data with DB_INPUT_TABLE then joining with EXT_HASH_JOIN.

name: Denormalizer
description:
Denormalizes input records - ie composes one output record from several input records using user-specified transformation. One group is defined with the same value on the given field(s) or has the fixed size. The transformation is supposed to implement interface RecordDenormalize.
Sends all resulting records to all connected output ports.
usage:
When to use: Collapse N consecutive input records into exactly 1 output record per group. Simpler than ROLLUP for the pure N→1 case.
Constraints: Input MUST be sorted on key. Exactly one output record per group (unlike ROLLUP which can emit multiple). Use ROLLUP if you need to emit multiple output records per group or need more complex state.

name: Rollup
description: Receives data through connected input port, groups incoming records by their key values and lets user transformation arbitrarily create new data records from the group by aggregating, composing or splitting. Sends these new data records through the connected output port(s) as specified by the transformation. Rollup function is superset for (De)Normalizer components.
usage:
When to use: Custom group processing logic that AGGREGATE can't express — e.g. conditional aggregation, producing multiple output records per group, or accumulating complex state. Full CTL2 control via initGroup/updateGroup/finishGroup/transform functions.
Constraints: Requires CTL2 code. Input must be sorted on groupKeyFields by default.
More complex to write than AGGREGATE. Use AGGREGATE if built-in aggregation functions cover the need.

name: LookupJoin
shortDescription: Joins incoming data flow with records from lookup table.
description: Receives data through connected input port (drivers), for each join key value contained in driver data records searches corresponding data records with the same key value in lookup table (slaves). Every pair of driver and slave records is sent to transformation class that joins them to form outgoing data flow. Resulting data records are sent to the first output port. Driver records without slave are sent to the second output port if connected and if inner join (default) is performed.
usage:
When to use: Join driver stream against a static lookup table already loaded into memory (defined in <Global>). Eliminates second input port. Best for small reference tables reused across multiple components in the same graph (lookup table loaded once, used many times).
Constraints: Lookup table must be defined in graph Global section. Not suitable for large or frequently-changing slave data.

name: FastSort
shortDescription: Sorts incoming data. This commercial version uses an improved version of external sort and is up to 2.5 times faster than ExtSort.
description: Receives data records through connected input port, sorts them according to specified sort key and sends them to output port. Sort key is name or combination of names of field(s) of incoming records. Sort order can be either ascending (default) or descending. Any number of records can be sorted. If internal buffer is full, external sorting is performed. FastSort can be up to 2.5 times faster than ExtSort. Can work well with international characters.
usage:
When to use: Dataset fits comfortably in memory. Significantly faster than EXT_SORT for in-memory data.
Constraints: No disk spill — will fail or degrade if data exceeds available RAM. Does not guarantee sort stability. Use EXT_SORT if size is of concern or sort stability is required.

name: MultiLevelReader

description:
Reads data from flat files containing heterogeneous data structure, identifies data records using a plugged selector
	    and sends individual groups of data records to corresponding connected output ports. Selector implements MultiLevelSelector interface. By default, PrefixMultiLevelSelector is used.

name: RelationalJoin
shortDescription: Joins data records that are not equal with respect to a given join key and operator.
description: Receives sorted data through both connected input ports, for each join key value contained in data records incoming through the first input port (drivers) searches corresponding data records incoming through the second input port (slaves) with the key values specified by one of the five possible join relation operators (e.g., non-equal, greater than, etc.). Thus, joining is performed based on these relations instead the exact matching required in the other joiners. For each join relation, sort order is strictly defined and must be the same for both inputs. Every combination of driver and its corresponding slaves is sent to transformation class that joins them to form outgoing data flow. Resulting records are sent to connected output port.
usage:
When to use: Non-equality join conditions (range, inequality, BETWEEN-style). e.g. join records where date falls within an interval.
Constraints: Both inputs must be sorted. Very specialized — use equality joiners for standard key-based joins.

name: ParallelReader
description:
Reads data from flat files using specified number of threads and sends data records to the first connected output port.
It supports both delimited and fixed-length data. However, it cannot handle fixed-length data encoded in variable-length charset (e.g. UTF-8). 
If "Data policy" is set to "Controlled" and an error occurs during processing of the input, the report is sent to the second port.
There are to options for selection of the parsing algorithm. The faster one can be used if certain features of the component are turned off and the record metadata conform to certain limitations. The other algorithm is more robust and can be used for any data supported by CloverDX. The component chooses the algorithm according to metadata and component parameters. The faster algorithm is chosen whenever possible. However, one of component parameters provides to the user the possibility to force the use of the robust algorithm.

When to use: Smaller or medium-sized JSON files where the entire document fits comfortably in memory (DOM parser). Complex or irregular JSON structure that is more naturally expressed with JSONPath than with CloverDX Mappings XML syntax.
Constraints: Loads the entire document into RAM — avoid for large files (practical limit ~5–10 MB depending on available heap). Use JSON_EXTRACT instead for large files or when hierarchical multi-port extraction with parentKey/generatedKey is needed.

description: Receives data through connected input port, splits incoming data flow into more flows to ensure all output ports are balanced employed.

Constraints: No content awareness — do NOT use when record destination must depend on field values. Use PARTITION for content-based routing.
